description: Coverage rules and COB calculation logic for both plans. Drives the COB engine in app/services/cob.py.
regulatory_basis: IRDAI (Insurance Products) Regulations 2024. IRDAI Health Dept FAQ on multiple policies.

cob_framework.principle: No-profit / indemnity principle: combined reimbursement from all policies cannot exceed actual admissible expenses
cob_framework.primary_insurer_selection: Policyholder's choice per IRDAI revised Master Circular Sep 2024. Can nominate any active indemnity policy as primary.
cob_framework.rationale: Aarav selects Plan B as primary because Plan B's per-claim deductible (₹25k) is lower than Plan A's aggregate deductible (₹50k) for a single large claim, maximising primary coverage. Priya selects Plan A as primary because Plan A has a higher PT sub-limit (₹30k vs ₹20k) and longer post-discharge window (90 vs 60 days).

step1_deductible.logic: Subtract deductible from total admissible claim. If total claim <= 50000, insurer pays nothing; patient pays full amount. Deductible is shared across all claims in the policy year. If deductible_already_met > 0, remaining_deductible = max(0, 50000 - deductible_already_met).

step2_coinsurance.logic: On amount above deductible: insurer_payment = (claim - remaining_deductible) * 0.80. Patient coinsurance share = (claim - remaining_deductible) * 0.20.

step3_oop_max_check.logic: Track cumulative patient payments (deductible + coinsurance). If cumulative_patient_payment >= 200000, insurer covers 100% of further admissible claims for rest of policy year.

step4_copay.notes: No co-payment for members under 60 at network hospitals.

pt_sub_limit.logic: PT reimbursement is capped at ₹30,000 regardless of actual spend. Sub-limit is applied before coinsurance: insurer pays min(actual_PT_cost, 30000) * 0.80.

step1_deductible.logic: Each new hospitalisation episode: patient pays first ₹25,000. If claim <= 25000, insurer pays nothing. When acting as secondary insurer, deductible is applied to the residual balance from primary settlement, not original total claim.

step2_coinsurance.logic: On amount above deductible: insurer_payment = (claim_or_residual - per_claim_deductible) * 0.75.

step3_copay_non_network.logic: At non-network hospitals, additional 10% co-payment applied on admissible amount after deductible: effective_insurer_payment = (claim - deductible) * 0.75 * (1 - 0.10). Or equivalently: patient pays deductible + 25% coinsurance + 10% copay on (claim - deductible).

step4_oop_max_check.logic: Cumulative patient payments tracked annually. Once ₹1,50,000 reached, insurer pays 100% of further claims.

pt_sub_limit.logic: PT reimbursement capped at ₹20,000. Applied before coinsurance: insurer pays min(actual_PT_cost, 20000) * 0.75.

worked_example_aarav_surgery.description: ACL Reconstruction + Meniscectomy. Total bill: ₹4,50,000. Plan B is primary, Plan A is secondary.

worked_example_priya_pt.description: Physiotherapy (CPT 97161 + 97110). Total PT bill: ₹30,000. Plan A is primary, Plan B is secondary.

primary_calculation.note: If Priya has not met her Plan A deductible yet, the full ₹30,000 falls within the deductible and Plan A pays ₹0. Patient pays ₹30,000.

secondary_calculation.note: Residual ₹6,000 is less than Plan B's per-claim deductible of ₹25,000. Plan B pays ₹0. Patient bears the full ₹6,000 residual.
